description_long: This sculpture depicts the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head and a human body, standing on one leg atop a round, lotus-like pedestal. The figure is adorned with a detailed headdress and halo, and holds various symbolic objects in its four hands, including an axe and a sweet. The craftsmanship features intricate carvings on the clothing, jewelry, and facial features, giving the piece a sense of depth and cultural richness. The surface has a weathered, brownish-gold patina, suggesting it is made from carved wood or cast resin with a faux-bronze finish. The back reveals the hollow halo and the detailed folds of the garment. The overall form is slender and vertical, with a stable, rounded base.
description: A detailed sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, standing on one leg atop a lotus pedestal, with intricate carvings and a brownish-gold finish.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing Ganesha's face, headdress, and all four arms holding symbolic objects, with the full pedestal visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the headdress, the profile of the trunk, and the layered garments.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the hollow halo, the back of the headdress, and the detailed folds of the clothing.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the face, trunk, and the raised leg, as well as the side of the pedestal.
attribute_subject_matter: Hindu deity Ganesha standing on a lotus pedestal
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
